nightcrawler 2025-12-28 9:17 p.m.
Forwarded
Greetings earthlings! Here is a note to let everyone know that BirminghamMesh has moved away from Meshtastic, in favor of Meshcore. I am new to this server, but have been working with Meshcore (and Reticulum) for several months and really like what I see. About half of our existing mesh repeaters in high places have migrated as of earlier this morning, with the remaining core repeaters expected to migrate in the next few days/weeks. We had hoped to stick with the USA standard, but its Spread Factor of 7 would not work for the existing distances and hilly environment. Perhaps at some point in the future, we may move to SF 7 once additional repeaters come online to fill in the gaps. BhamMesh config params: 907.375 MHz, SF 9, CR 5 We picked the 907.375 MHz frequency after watching traffic over several days, but also because it falls within the passband of the already deployed RF cavities, which saves time and effort... Happy New Year!
Originally sent: 2025-12-28 6:33 p.m. (edited)
9:17 p.m.
Looks like they are going with a custom setup with SF9 as well but also a custom frequency

"Sunrise, Sunset" is a song from the musical Fiddler on the Roof written in 1964 by composer Jerry Bock and lyricist Sheldon Harnick. The song is performed at the wedding of Tzeitel (Tevye and Golde's eldest daughter). Scott Redd 2025-12-28 11:14 p.m.
Hey, Folks! I've made some updates to the content at http://OmaMesh.net. Your feedback is welcome. Also, if you'd like to contribute, there's a blog function available. Post here or message me if you'd like to get started. I know that many of you have stories to share. Yes, I've used some AI to help me organize and write this. Let me know if something looks dumb, need more context, or is just plain wrong.
Home | OmaMesh
Metro Omaha mesh community on Meshtastic and MeshCore.

Scott Redd
Hey, Folks! I've made some updates to the content at http://OmaMesh.net. Your feedback is welcome. Also, if you'd like to contribute, there's a blog function available. Post here or message me if you'd like to get started. I know that many of you have stories to share. Yes, I've used some AI to help me organize and write this. Let me know if something looks dumb, need more context, or is just plain wrong.
amc4550 2025-12-28 11:28 p.m.
Looks good, thanks for all the hard work on this!

Caboose 2025-12-28 11:29 p.m.
Aside from that it looks great
nightcrawler
@too3zln If you didn't already know, this is a great way to see if your packets make it all the way to Omaha - https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/packets
amc4550 2025-12-28 11:36 p.m.
Pretty cool... this could be useful in analyzing gaps in the mesh. Do I see correctly that NC2 and Valley are the only nodes uploading data at the moment? (edited)
Caboose
Seems pedantic I'm sure but some people might wait 10 minutes then panic
Scott Redd 2025-12-28 11:37 p.m.
Good point. Patience is a virtue. I changed it to: Your first steps on the mesh Once your device is configured: Power it on and let it run for a few minutes Watch for node discovery - you should start seeing nearby stations. It make take a few hours depending on the advert frequency of companion nodes, repeaters, and room servers. Find nearby repeaters: in your companion app, navigate to Tools -> Discover Nearby Nodes -> Discover Repeaters. This will ask local repeaters to identify themselves to you. Send a short message in the public channel and look for repeats or even replies from other users Try sending a direct message (DM) to another node Look for a delivery acknowledgement (ACK) If you get an ACK, congratulations - you are on the mesh. (edited)
2
amc4550
Pretty cool... this could be useful in analyzing gaps in the mesh. Do I see correctly that NC2 and Valley are the only nodes uploading data at the moment? (edited)

Good point. Patience is a virtue. I changed it to: Your first steps on the mesh Once your device is configured: Power it on and let it run for a few minutes Watch for node discovery - you should start seeing nearby stations. It make take a few hours depending on the advert frequency of companion nodes, repeaters, and room servers. Find nearby repeaters: in your companion app, navigate to Tools -> Discover Nearby Nodes -> Discover Repeaters. This will ask local repeaters to identify themselves to you. Send a short message in the public channel and look for repeats or even replies from other users Try sending a direct message (DM) to another node Look for a delivery acknowledgement (ACK) If you get an ACK, congratulations - you are on the mesh. (edited)

Scott Redd
I get where you are going with the standards for naming, but that could take the ad-hoc fun out of it. And some folks may never delve deep into it enough to understand why it’s a good idea. Scott Redd 2025-12-29 11:32 a.m.
While Blair isn't really metro Omaha (neither in Lincoln), it's still important, and neat enough that it would be way cool to build out a larger mesh that bridges anything we can. I will add a page to the site at http://omamesh.net about mesh building in other areas in the region and hopefully folks looking for info online will come across it and join us.
Home | OmaMesh
Metro Omaha mesh community on Meshtastic and MeshCore.
11:37 a.m.
To @Tom (Blair) , @Brandon-CBS1 and anyone else working at the edges to build out and link to the larger mesh from more remote fringes, you are invited to write content for publication on the web site as a blog. That might help folks find you and join your efforts.
Scott Redd
To @Tom (Blair) , @Brandon-CBS1 and anyone else working at the edges to build out and link to the larger mesh from more remote fringes, you are invited to write content for publication on the web site as a blog. That might help folks find you and join your efforts.

[AL0R] Matt 2025-12-29 7:57 p.m.
Let's say.... it's "up there"....
7:57 p.m.

7:58 p.m.
So keep that in mind when you are looking for the "best" antenna... it will still work
Pyromaniac3814 2025-12-29 7:58 p.m.
But, why not put the outright best on mead
[AL0R] Matt 2025-12-29 7:58 p.m.
In this case, altitude is the tradeoff for a suboptimal antenna.
Pyromaniac3814
But, why not put the outright best on mead
[AL0R] Matt 2025-12-29 7:59 p.m.

8:00 p.m.

Scott Redd 2025-12-30 11:14 p.m.
Here’s an idea: use the solar JST port on the board to accept a really lightweight pigtail that comes out of the flip top. Terminate that with JST. Then make a custom USB to JST for charging.

It fits! Just barely. It’s tight enough I didn’t need any kind of insert to keep it from shifting inside. Had to orient the board plus JST in order to make proper use of the space. I made a polarity swapping cable and I’m sure my solder joints are sus.
timmo_3.14 2025-12-31 3:16 p.m.
Be aware that any pokey solder joints can pretty easily pierce a lipo pouch. I did a "thing" this past weekend that made me run a build out of the house while pulling off wires. I recommend a piece of plastic sheeting between the rak and lipo . (edited)
2
Scott Redd
Gotta double check the power requirements of the solar connector though. I think it’s pretty forgiving.
timmo_3.14 2025-12-31 3:22 p.m.
I do know that the rak can only charge the battery at max 350mA. You can have more than that at the solar input, but max charging is 350mA.
Scott Redd 2025-12-31 3:23 p.m.
But it won't hurt it thought, right? If I tie on a 0.5A 5v feed into the solar port, it'll just regulate to 350?
timmo_3.14 2025-12-31 4:25 p.m.
Correct. I had a 1A 5v (5 watt) and it was fine - made sure I had that max charge on cloudy days. (edited)
AstroBob 2025-12-31 4:41 p.m.
If you want a bigger battery, you can just by-pass the on-board solar and source your own solar controller to charge the battery separately, Then, you can overpanel to suit your needs and cloud cover.
timmo_3.14 2025-12-31 4:42 p.m.
What Bob said! This is how I run LSR and WSR. Solar to 1 amp charge controller directly to entirely too much lipo. (edited)
1
AstroBob 2025-12-31 4:46 p.m.
Here's a tool that I use frequently to position my panels for maximum efficiency. https://www.suncalc.org

Application for determining the course of the sun at a desired time and place with interactive map.

timmo_3.14 2026-01-01 5:21 p.m.

USB c
Effortlessly charge and maintain your 18650 lithium batteries with our 15-pack TP4056 Charging Module, featuring a Type-C USB input for modern convenience. Each module delivers a steady 1A current, with a charging cut-off at 4.2V, ensuring optimal battery care. The power indicator lights keep you...
